channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Middle East latest: A long-awaited Gaza ceasefire starts after a delay
item.description: A ceasefire in the Gaza Strip took effect on Sunday following an almost three-hour delay after Hamas was late to name the three hostage it plans to release. The announcement from Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office said the ceasefire began at 11:15 a.m. local time (0915 GMT).
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: TikTok goes dark as U.S. ban takes effect
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Millions of TikTok users in the United States are no longer able to watch videos on the social media platform as a federal ban on the immensely popular app takes effect.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Taliban deputy tells leader there is no excuse for education bans on Afghan women and girls
item.description: A senior Taliban figure has urged the group's leader to scrap education bans on Afghan women and girls, saying there is no excuse for them, in a rare public rebuke of government policy.
item.dc:creator: THE ASSOCIATED PRESS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Travis Kelce makes NFL history in first game of three-peat quest
item.description: Once again, Travis Kelce has broken a record at Arrowhead Stadium after catching a pass from Patrick Mahomes.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, KTLA 5

item.title: Families of wildfire victims mired in grief, questioning what more could have been done
item.description: Many families in California are full of grief and questions about what more they could have done to save their loved ones from the deadly wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Heather Hollingsworth And Mark Thiessen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The rise
item.description: The possibility of the U.S. outlawing TikTok kept influencers and users in anxious limbo during the four-plus years that lawmakers and judges debated the fate of the video-sharing app. Now, the moment its fans dreaded is here, but uncertainty over TikTok’s future lingers.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Missouri lawmakers are going after voter-approved abortion rights. Voters will likely reelect them
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — Voters in Missouri last election approved a constitutional amendment that promised to undo the state's near-total abortion ban.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Legacy news on its heels as second Trump era begins, but not abandoning mission
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As Donald Trump prepares to assume the presidency for a second time, he faces a news establishment on its heels — but not flat on its back. Last year's presidential campaign suggested that many traditional outlets are not depended upon, or trusted, nearly as much as they used to be.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US cities largely saw a drop in violent crime in 2024, yet many still feel unsafe
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — As U.S. police departments release preliminary or finalized 2024 crime numbers, many are reporting historic declines in homicides and drops in other violent crimes compared to 2023. In many parts of the country, though, those decreases don't match the public perception.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California officials warn against price gouging as rents soar in fire-stricken Los Angeles
item.description: Southern California's expensive housing market is going to get a lot more competitive after deadly firestorms torched more than 12,000 homes and other structures in the Los Angeles area, leaving tens of thousands of people without a place to stay.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Washington retires its first women's jersey, sending Kelsey Plum's No. 10 to the rafters
item.description: It was always a matter of when — not if — Washington would retire Kelsey Plum’s number. Eight years after she graduated, it happened.
item.dc:creator: JOSH KIRSHENBAUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Extreme cold forecast for much of US in coming days
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — Another blast of winter weather is expected in parts of the U.S. in the coming days, including bone-chilling wind in the Northern Plains and unusual snow and ice in the Gulf Coast area. Cold weather forecasted for Monday for Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC mayor defends private meeting with incoming President Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City's Democratic mayor, who faces federal bribery charges, is defending his private meeting with President-elect Donald Trump as the Republican prepares to retake the White House on Monday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Live updates: Trump inauguration weekend commences as protesters rally in Washington
item.description: Inauguration festivities will begin in full force Saturday as President-elect Trump and his allies — and protesters — arrive in Washington ahead of Monday's inauguration of the 47th president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia appeals court sides with Trump in affirming dismissal of 6 state charges
item.description: A Georgia appeals court on Friday affirmed the dismissal of six counts in the state's 2020 election interference against President-elect Trump and his allies, upholding a trial judge's decision that the charges must be thrown out due to a lack of detail.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Menendez brothers resentencing hearing postponed due to raging Los Angeles fires
item.description: The resentencing hearing for the Menendez Brothers, who notoriously shot their parents to death in 1989, is being pushed back by nearly two months due to California’s devastating wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Homes were burning and roads already jammed when Pacific Palisades evacuation order came, AP finds
item.description: The first evacuation order covering neighborhoods closest to the start of the devastating Pacific Palisades wildfire didn’t come until about 40 minutes after some of those homes were already burning, according to an Associated Press analysis of emergency communications and interviews with survivors.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, GENE JOHNSON, CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, CLAUDIA LAUER and AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First responders saved a moose that fell through lake ice in New York. Here's how they did it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A bull moose that fell through lake ice in the Adirondack Mountains was saved by New York conservation officials in a laborious cold-water rescue. The moose fell through the ice around 11 a.m.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Children's book author and engineer husband exposed for double life in house of horrors
item.description: After one of their adopted children was hospitalized following harrowing tales of abuse, her parents, a children's author and her husband, were handed lengthy prison sentences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Carolina man pleads guilty to the carjacking and killing of a New Mexico police officer
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A South Carolina man pleaded guilty Friday to charges that he gunned down a New Mexico state police officer who had stopped to help him, marking a turning point in a case that had been set for trial later this year.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hollywood's awards season dates, and how they've been affected by the wildfires
item.description: Hollywood's awards season has all but come to a rare halt as the wildfires continue to disrupt life and work in the Los Angeles area. There are near daily updates from the Hollywood guilds and organizations that put on awards shows as the industry navigates the ongoing crisis.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Wisdom of a California grandmother, 90, goes viral amid Eaton Fire tragedy
item.description: A nearly 90-year-old California woman who lost her home to the Eaton Fire is going viral for pragmatism and positivity in the face of so much adversity.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man accused of woman's disappearance, murder previously charged with attempted murder, kidnapping
item.description: Fayetteville, North Carolina, authorities on Thursday arrested Tyrell Siermons, 30, in connection with the January disappearance and murder of 25-year-old Heather Williams.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fires scorched campuses across Los Angeles. Many schools are seeking places to hold classes
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Days after losing her home in the same fire that destroyed her Los Angeles elementary school, third-grader Gabriela Chevez-Muñoz resumed classes this week at another campus temporarily hosting children from her school.
item.dc:creator: KRYSTA FAURIA, JOCELYN GECKER and CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former Oakland mayor, longtime partner indicted in alleged bribery scheme
item.description: OAKLAND (AP) — Former Oakland Mayor Sheng Thao, her longtime partner and two local businessmen were named in an indictment unsealed Friday alleging the four conspired to funnel city business to companies owned by the men in exchange for money and benefits to Thao and her partner.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman mistakenly declared dead after funeral home's typo
item.description: A Maryland woman's life was turned upside down after a simple typo led to her being declared legally dead.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Engineer Tedesco brothers use retrofitted RV to track UAPs
item.description: Two brothers have retrofitted an RV into a mobile lab to collect data about UAPs and other mysterious flying objects.
item.dc:creator: Laura Ingle, NewsNation

item.title: Sealy Mattress company is sued after Georgia man was found dead inside a locked trailer
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A Georgia man suffering a mental health crisis wandered into a Sealy Mattress facility, and his body was found locked inside the back of a truck there about a week later, his family says in a wrongful death lawsuit filed this week.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Will spring have showers? NOAA releases weather predictions for California
item.description: A La Niña winter just started, but it isn't expected to last long. National forecasters are already looking ahead to the spring season.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Ramaswamy plans run for Ohio governor
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy, the co-chair of President-elect Trump's new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), plans to run for governor of Ohio, two sources confirmed to The Hill on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Capital One technical issue delays payments for users: What to know
item.description: Some Capital One users were still waiting to receive deposits and payments on Friday morning after the company experienced a processing disruption.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mel Gibson ‘just as surprised’ as anyone to be named Trump ‘special envoy’ to Hollywood
item.description: Actor and film producer Mel Gibson said he was stunned to be appointed a special envoy to Hollywood under the upcoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Pepsi discontinues 'permanent' flavor, debuts a completely different one
item.description: Pepsi is planning to debut a brand-new flavor this month, marking its first “permanent” addition to the brand’s beverage portfolio in four years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Higher stakes stoke passions at DNC forum
item.description: With just over 2 weeks until the DNC chooses new officers, candidates for chair know stakes have been raised.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences of nearly 2,500 non-violent drug offenders
item.description: Biden said he is proud of his record on clemency and will “continue to review additional commutations and pardons” before he leaves office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Official portraits of Donald Trump, JD Vance are released
item.description: Not waiting until after his inauguration, on Thursday President-elect Trump gave the country a look at what he says are the official portraits of himself and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Jeff Bezos’ New Glenn rocket reaches orbit on first test flight
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — Blue Origin launched its massive new rocket on its first test flight Thursday, sending up a prototype satellite to orbit thousands of miles above Earth.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, ABC 4
